subject: Public Health
title: Secondary Prevention and Screening
description: A 60-second lesson on Secondary Prevention and Screening.

hook.text: Secondary prevention catches disease early—before symptoms appear and damage is done.

buildup.text: Mammograms, blood pressure checks, and HIV testing are all forms of screening.

discovery.text: Catching cervical cancer early through Pap smears cut the death rate by 70% over four decades.

twist.text: Screening can also harm—false positives cause anxiety, unnecessary procedures, and overdiagnosis.

climax.text: Good screening programs balance sensitivity with specificity to minimize both misses and false alarms.

punchline.text: Find it early and the odds flip in your favor.

quiz.question: What is a risk of population-wide screening?
options: False positives can cause anxiety and unnecessary procedures
